about.label: About
about.title: Developer with a CTO background
about.lead: I build and run web applications, from backend code to technology decisions.
paragraphs: Web developer with experience leading engineering teams as a CTO. I write code and I make technology choices that fit the business.
I work with startups and established companies that need senior help without a full-time hire: shipping features, reviewing architecture, or getting through a tough migration.

services.label: Services
services.title: How I can help
services.lead: Engagement models that match where your team is right now.

items.title: Fractional Developer
items.summary: Senior hands-on work when you need extra capacity.

items.title: Fractional CTO
items.summary: Technology leadership without a full-time role.

stack.label: Stack
stack.title: Technologies I work with
stack.lead: Ruby is where I'm most at home. I choose tools that fit the problem, not the other way around.

primary.name: Ruby
primary.tip: Fast to build and easy to change. A strong fit for MVPs and products that need to evolve quickly.

primary.name: Rails
primary.tip: Ships features with less glue code. Lower maintenance cost and a large pool of developers.

primary.name: PostgreSQL
primary.tip: Reliable data storage that scales with the business. No need to swap databases early.

primary.name: Redis
primary.tip: Keeps the app fast under load. Handles caching and short-lived data without slowing users down.

primary.name: Sidekiq
primary.tip: Background jobs for emails, reports, and imports. The app stays responsive while work runs async.

also.name: TypeScript
also.tip: Catches bugs before release. Safer refactors as the codebase and team grow.

also.name: Node.js
also.tip: Good for APIs and tooling when the team already works in JavaScript.

also.name: Python
also.tip: Useful for integrations, data tasks, and connecting to AI or ML services.

also.name: Docker
also.tip: Same setup in dev and production. Fewer surprises when you deploy.

also.name: AWS
also.tip: Hosting that grows with the product. Turn services on when you need them, not before.

stack.note: Happy to join your existing stack or help pick the right one for a new project.

expertise.label: Expertise
expertise.title: Where I focus
expertise.lead: From overall system design down to specific problems. Hover a tag to see what I actually do in each area.

items.name: System architecture
items.tip: I design systems from scratch or steer existing ones so you can add features without rewriting, server costs stay reasonable, and scaling is straightforward when traffic grows.

items.name: Team leadership
items.tip: I help shape the team, agree on how you write and review code, and mentor people so shipping does not hinge on one developer.

items.name: Backend development
items.tip: I write the core application code: business logic, databases, background jobs. The layer that has to work reliably every day.

items.name: API design
items.tip: I design APIs your frontend and partners can build against without surprises as the product grows.

items.name: Monitoring & data
items.tip: I set up monitoring so we are not guessing what happens in production. Bugs surface fast, trends show up in the data, and we track SLOs and spot bottlenecks before users feel them.

items.name: Legacy migrations
items.tip: I move old codebases forward step by step. The business keeps running, risk goes down, no big-bang rewrite.

items.name: AI-assisted development
items.tip: I use AI where it saves me real time, and I review what goes to production so quality does not slip.

howIWork.label: Process
howIWork.title: How I work
howIWork.lead: Business goals first, then the technical plan.

steps.title: Assess
steps.text: Look at the codebase, the team, the constraints, and what success means for the business.

steps.title: Plan
steps.text: Agree on a technical approach with realistic milestones. No unnecessary complexity.

steps.title: Deliver
steps.text: Regular updates, working software, and knowledge passed on to your team.

contact.label: Contact
contact.title: Let's talk
contact.lead: Working on something or want to see if we fit? Send a message.
contact.name: Name
contact.mailHint: Your email app will open — send the message from there.
